evidence: Verified Roulette 1971 tracklist is the 5 tracks listed in after; 'Azucar' is not on this album (it is an Eddie Palmieri solo track from Azucar Pa' Ti).

'Mystic Brew' is track 7 of Ronnie Foster's 1972 Blue Note album 'Two Headed Freap'; this is the Wikipedia article for the actual album (title field still says 'Mystic Brew' -- flagged for separate title fix).
English Wikipedia article for The Real Book, the c.1975 bootleg Berklee jazz fake book of lead sheets for standards.
Juice was recorded at RCA NYC May 5-7 1976 and originally released in 1976 (RCA Victor APL1-1855), not 1977.
Original 1976 US release was on RCA Victor (cat. APL1-1855); 'RVC' was the later Japanese distributor RVC Corp, not the original label.

Verified 7-track Juice tracklist (Raisins/Sometime/The Breeze And I/East Side Boogie/El Toro/Bamboo Child/Andes); 'Juice' is the album title not a track, and 'Little Tree' is not on the album -- both removed.

before: A jazz-funk outing from 1977, Juice features guitarist Ryo Kawasaki recording for RVC. The program includes "Juice", "Raisins", and "Bamboo Child". A cult classic of Japanese jazz-funk. Kawasaki's guitar synthesizer pioneering influenced later electronic jazz.
after: Guitarist Ryo Kawasaki recorded Juice at RCA in New York in May 1976; it was his first album for an American label, RCA Victor. The jazz-funk program ranges across originals like "Raisins" and "El Toro" and a reading of "The Breeze and I." Kawasaki was an early adopter of the guitar synthesizer, and the record became a sought-after title among crate-diggers and jazz-funk listeners.
evidence: Corrects the prior DNA's errors: wrong year (1977), wrong label (RVC), and a nonexistent track ('Juice'). New text uses only sourced facts -- 1976, RCA Victor, recorded NYC, first US-label release, guitar synth, real tracks.
English Wikipedia article for Ryo Fukui's 1977 Trio Records album Mellow Dream (artist, year, label and tracklist all confirmed against current data).
Verified 11-track Tetterettet tracklist (ICP 020, 1977); 'Brozziman' and 'Two Hoedown' are not on this album. The after-list gives 6 real tracks from the verified tracklist.
Chet Baker 'Live in Rosenheim' (rec. 17 Apr 1988) was released 1989 on Timeless Records (Du), cat. SJP 233.
